output_buffering_partial.problem: Test checked log while stalld running, output buffered
output_buffering_partial.solution: Moved stop_stalld() BEFORE log check to force buffer flush

Additional buffering layer in BPF/libbpf that doesn't flush on process exit
Timing race: check happens before buffers fully flushed to file

output_buffering_when_redirected.mechanism: Output redirected to file (> log 2>&1) is fully buffered
output_buffering_when_redirected.our_solution: Stop stalld to trigger process exit flush
output_buffering_when_redirected.implication: queue_track has additional buffering layer we haven't accounted for

queue_track_architecture.output_path: BPF → perf buffers → userspace → stdout/stderr → file
queue_track_architecture.more_layers: BPF has additional buffering compared to sched_debug's direct file parsing
queue_track_architecture.potential_issue: Perf buffers may not flush immediately on process exit

breakdown.buffer: Processing time (3s for parsing, detection, logging)

timing_formula_complete.worst_case: stalld checks just before threshold, must wait full granularity for next check

kernel_worker_behavior.problem: ksoftirqd and kworker on test CPU starve alongside test tasks
kernel_worker_behavior.why_first: Kernel workers exist before test starts, have earlier timestamps
kernel_worker_behavior.backend_specific: Only affects sched_debug (queue_track has FIFO limitation)

test_starvation_detection.sh.test3_line_260: stop_stalld() called here before checking log

lessons_learned_today.lesson_1: Same fix doesn't always work for both backends - they have different architectures
lessons_learned_today.lesson_2: BPF-based backends may need special handling for output flushing
lessons_learned_today.lesson_3: Process exit flush works for direct file I/O but not necessarily for BPF perf buffers
lessons_learned_today.lesson_4: Always test fixes on BOTH backends before considering them complete
